jobs.title: Computational Toxicologist Drug Discovery & Liver Safety
jobs.description:
Position: Computational Toxicologist for Drug Discovery & Liver Safety Axiom is building a computational toxicology platform and you will help establish the foundation for toxicity prediction using multimodal datasets and collaboration with ML researchers.You will analyze high-content imaging, transcriptomics, and proteomics from human hepatocytes and hepatic systems, develop robust features and QC, and bridge wet lab protocols with machine learning models to explain mechanisms and guide drug design.#J-18808-Ljbffr
Responsibilities
Axiom is building a computational toxicology platform and you will help establish the foundation for toxicity prediction using multimodal datasets and collaboration with ML researchers
You will analyze high-content imaging, transcriptomics, and proteomics from human hepatocytes and hepatic systems, develop robust features and QC, and bridge wet lab protocols with machine learning models to explain mechanisms and guide drug design

jobs.title: SENIOR ENVIRONMENTAL SCIENTIST (SUPERVISORY)
jobs.description:
Job Posting: SCE Supervisor



Department of Fish and Wildlife


JC-525540SCE Supervisor


SENIOR ENVIRONMENTAL SCIENTIST (SUPERVISORY)
$10,854.00 - $13,493.00 per Month


New to State candidates will be hired into the minimum salary of the classification or minimum of alternate range when applicable.


Final Filing Date: 8/4/2026


Job Description and Duties


Are you ready to take on a challenge and lead a dynamic team working on high-profile environmental projects? The California Department of Fish and Wildlife (CDFW) is seeking a Limited Term Senior Environmental Scientist (Supervisory) to oversee the Southern California Edison (SCE) workload in our Habitat Conservation Planning Program. If you are a self-motivated individual who thrives in fast-paced, complex work environments and is passionate about conservation and environmental protection, we want to hear from you!





About the Role: This is a working supervisor position where you will supervise and conduct environmental review, permitting, and conservation planning functions for SCE projects. You will play a key role in ensuring compliance with the California Endangered Species Act (CESA), California Environmental Quality Act (CEQA), and Lake and Streambed Alteration (LSA) agreements, working to maintain a balance between environmental protection and project progress.





CDFW may be serving as the Lead or Responsible Agency under CEQA in order to issue permits, in this role you will initiate, execute, and manage contracts with SCE, coordinate with consultants, and oversee the preparation of critical documents. You will guide and support the issuance of permits, ensure timely completion of environmental reviews, facilitate mitigation efforts, and assess the success of conservation measures across multiple regions.





What You Will Do:
- Lead a team of Senior Environmental Scientist (Specialists) in environmental review, permitting, and conservation planning for SCE projects.
- Oversee the development and certification of CEQA documents for SCE's permits, including a Master Lake and Streambed Alteration Agreement (LSAA) and possibly CESA incidental take permits for projects across Regions 4, 5, and 6.
- Coordinate with regional and statewide staff to ensure project goals are met on time and in compliance with environmental laws and regulations.
- Provide leadership and mentorship to your team, fostering a collaborative environment and driving success in complex, multi-regional projects.


What We Are Looking For: We need someone who can take on a challenge, manage multiple complex projects, and ensure deadlines are met while maintaining the highest standards of environmental stewardship. If you are a problem-solver with strong leadership skills and a passion for conservation, this could be the perfect role for you!





In order to be eligible for this position, you must either be transfer eligible or take and pass the following exam.

Class Title: Senior Environmental Scientist (Supervisory)/Exam Bulletin





This position is posted in the following counties: Orange/Santa Barbara/San Diego counties. Please apply to each county in which you are interested in working.





This position is a 12-month Limited Term (LT) and may be extended up to 24 months or become permanent.

During this 12-month Limited Term assignment, you will receive full health, dental, and vision benefits! You will also earn State Service and accrue monthly leave credits.









You will find additional information about the job in the Duty Statement.




Working Conditions
The office will be located in the Seal Beach, Santa Barbara or San Diego office and must be able to travel up to 20% to attend site visits throughout the South Coast Region, including Santa Barbara, Ventura, Los Angeles, and Orange Counties. Travel outside the region up to 5% will also be required. The position involves using a computer keyboard and Microsoft Office software for up to eight hours per day, as well as performing office tasks that require sitting, standing, and walking between locations. The role includes coordinating and participating in meetings and conference calls, attending in-person workgroups with stakeholders, and engaging with local, state, and federal agencies in the South Coast Region, as well as Regions 4 and 6. Field site visits will be necessary, and the incumbent must be able to walk on uneven, hilly terrain. As a condition of employment, the incumbent may be required to wear a uniform in accordance with CDFW’s Operations Manual (Section 12901) during field surveys and public interactions, both within and outside the Region as directed by the supervisor. A valid driver's license is required travel and field site visits to remote or rural areas where alternative transportation is not feasible. Telework is up to the discretion and approval of the supervisor and will be consistent with current telework policies.



Position Details
Job Code #:
JC-525540
Position #(s):
565-531-0764-905
Working Title:
SCE Supervisor
Classification:
SENIOR ENVIRONMENTAL SCIENTIST (SUPERVISORY)
$10,854.00 - $13,493.00
New to State candidates will be hired into the minimum salary of the classification or minimum of alternate range when applicable.
# of Positions:
1
Work Location:
Orange County
Telework:
Hybrid
Job Type:
12 Month Limited Term - Full Time
Department Information
Department of Fish and Wildlife (CDFW) employees are committed to managing and protecting California’s diverse wildlife and the habitats upon which they depend.

California is one of the most biodiverse places on the planet. As such, CDFW values diverse employees working together to protect nature for all Californians. CDFW is committed to fostering an inclusive work environment where all backgrounds, cultures and personal experiences can thrive and connect others to our critical mission.

Join us and begin a meaningful new career!
Department Website: https://www.wildlife.ca.gov
Application Instructions
Co

jobs.title: Nurse Practitioner PRN - Health Risk Assessments
jobs.description:
Hueman is actively hiring Nurse Practitioners to complete in‑home Health Risk Assessments (HRAs) for Medicare and Medicaid members in and around South San Francisco, CA.
This unique opportunity adapts to your lifestyle, giving you the freedom to achieve your professional goals on your terms, all while enjoying a schedule designed with your preferences in mind.
Whether it be for supplemental income or the desire for community connection, this position invites you to become an integral part of the thriving HRA landscape, fostering connections that go beyond the clinic.
Position Summary Qualified Nurse Practitioners interested in this position will have the opportunity to interact with members in a more intimate setting.
Unlike a typical clinic visit, this role allows NPs to connect with patients on a more personal and effective level.
Nurse Practitioners performing HRAs are not required to treat or prescribe in the home.
Nurse Practitioner Responsibilities Travel to and from patients' homes.
Perform comprehensive, non‑invasive physical assessments.
Review medical history and ensure accurate and timely documentation.
Conduct medical reconciliation to ensure patient safety and compliance.
Provide basic patient education on wellness and preventative healthcare measures.
Nurse Practitioner Qualifications and Experience Requirements Master's Degree or commensurate experience and satisfactory completion of NP license.
Active State NP license.
Active National board certification.
Current BLS certification.
12 months of experience as a paid advanced clinical provider is preferred but not required.
Benefits Competitive compensation and travel reimbursement.
Flexible schedule.
Comprehensive home assessment training.
Dedicated patient scheduling assistance.
Liability insurance options.
To learn more about this position, please use this link to schedule a call with a member of our recruitment team.
About Hueman:
 At Hueman, we're dedicated to guiding you toward fulfilling career opportunities.
We believe that happiness should be a fundamental part of every role in risk assessment.
Our opportunities provide flexibility, extra income, and integration into your local healthcare network.
If this resonates with your idea of career satisfaction, allow us to assist you in discovering your next healthcare position!
•

Active State NP license

Current BLS certification

This unique opportunity adapts to your lifestyle, giving you the freedom to achieve your professional goals on your terms, all while enjoying a schedule designed with your preferences in mind
Benefits Competitive compensation and travel reimbursement
Flexible schedule
Comprehensive home assessment training
Dedicated patient scheduling assistance

Unlike a typical clinic visit, this role allows NPs to connect with patients on a more personal and effective level

jobs.title: Environmental Health Specialist I
jobs.description:
Position Information
Continue to grow your career in Environmental Health and join the Department of Health Services as an Environmental Health Specialist I!
Starting salary up to $55.98/hour ($116,836/year) and a competitive total compensation package!*
The Department of Health Services is currently recruiting to fill one full-time Environmental Health Specialist (EHS) position at either the Trainee, I, or II level. If you are interested in being considered at the different levels of this opportunity, you must apply to the separate concurrent recruitments being conducted for each level.


Please Note: As mandated by the State of California's Health and Safety Code, the Environmental Health Specialist I level requires possession of a valid certificate of registration as an Environmental Health Specialist. For more information on the requirements and process to obtain a Registered Environmental Health Specialist certificate in California, please visit the CA Registered Environmental Health Specialist Program website.


About the Position
We're looking for a motivated individual to join our team as an Environmental Health Specialist I who wants to being a career in Environmental Health with the County of Sonoma. This role is vital to protecting public health, serving the community, and supporting the functions of the Environmental Health Section. As an Environmental Health Specialist I, you will:

Perform inspections and investigations across a wide range of programs, including but not limited to: Food Facilities, Recreational Facilities, Cannabis Facilities, Medical Waste Facilities, Water Systems, Beach monitoring, Solid Waste, Body Art, Childhood Lead Prevention, Storm Water, Organized Camps, Detention Facilities, and Disaster Preparedness
Ensure compliance with County and State health and safety regulations through routine inspections, complaint responses, documentation, enforcement actions, and public education
Prepare thorough inspection reports, interpret relevant health codes, and communicate findings and corrective actions to stakeholders
Interact regularly with the public, businesses, and regulatory partners, often providing guidance and support in diverse environments

As the ideal candidate for this position, you will be dedicated to helping promote a healthy and safe environment, be an excellent communicator, and possess many of the following:

Experience with sanitation science, public health principles, and relevant environmental health laws and regulations
The ability to adapt to an evolving public health landscape and perform a broad range of functions to meet Departmental operational needs
Experience identifying potential issues, researching these issues, preparing supporting documentation, and presenting these observations accurately and effectively
Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Word, Outlook, Excel, PowerPoint) and general computer skills
Effective written and verbal communication skills, including report writing, documentation, and presentations
The ability to think critically, research regulatory issues, and analyze field findings
A strong sense of empathy for the clients you will work with
The ability to build collaborative relationships with a diverse community, team members, and partner agencies

Work is primarily performed Monday through Friday, with occasional evenings and weekends to inspect temporary events or respond to complaints. Many inspections are outdoors and require working in varying environmental conditions, including beaches, waste sites, and food service facilities.
What We Offer
Working at the County of Sonoma offers expansive opportunities for growth and development, the ability to be a part of a challenging and rewarding work environment, and the satisfaction of knowing you're working to better our communities. You can also look forward to flexible work arrangements and excellent benefits* including:

Salary Advancement - A salary increase after 1,040 hours (6 months when working full-time) for good work performance; eligibility for a salary increase for good performance every year thereafter, until reaching the top of the salary range

Career Advancement - With time and good performance, the opportunity for advancement to Environmental Health Specialist II ($49.80 - $60.53/hour)

Paid Time Off - Competitive vacation and sick leave accruals and 12 paid holidays

County Paid Health Premium Contributions - 100% premium contribution for the majority of employee-only and employee + family health plan options

Staff Development/Wellness - Annual benefit allowances of up to $850 and
Qualifications
Experience with sanitation science, public health principles, and relevant environmental health laws and regulations
The ability to adapt to an evolving public health landscape and perform a broad range of functions to meet Departmental operational needs
Experience identifying potential issues, researching these issues, preparing supporting documentation, and presenting these observations accurately and effectively
Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Word, Outlook, Excel, PowerPoint) and general computer skills
Effective written and verbal communication skills, including report writing, documentation, and presentations
The ability to think critically, research regulatory issues, and analyze field findings
A strong sense of empathy for the clients you will work with
The ability to build collaborative relationships with a diverse community, team members, and partner agencies
Benefits
Starting salary up to $55.98/hour ($116,836/year) and a competitive total compensation package!*
Salary Advancement - A salary increase after 1,040 hours (6 months when working full-time) for good work performance; eligibility for a salary increase for good performance every year thereafter, until reaching the top of the salary range
Career Advancement - With time and good performance, the opportunity for advancement to Environmental Health Specialist II ($49.80 - $60.53/hour)
Paid

jobs.title: On-site Environmental Compliance Leader
jobs.description:
Hims & Hers is seeking an Environmental Compliance Specialist to serve as the on‑site lead at our Menlo Park, CA facility. You will manage hazardous waste, air quality, wastewater, stormwater, and regulatory reporting with a high degree of independence.

You will report to senior environmental leadership and coordinate with regulators, vendors, and operations to ensure full compliance and continuous improvement in line with California requirements.
Responsibilities
You will manage hazardous waste, air quality, wastewater, stormwater, and regulatory reporting with a high degree of independence
You will report to senior environmental leadership and coordinate with regulators, vendors, and operations to ensure full compliance and continuous improvement in line with California requirements

jobs.title: Environmental Specialist I
jobs.description:
Company Description

Veolia in North America is the top-ranked environmental company in the United States for three consecutive years, and the country’s largest private water operator and technology provider as well as hazardous waste and pollution treatment leader. It offers a full spectrum of water, waste, and energy management services, including water and wastewater treatment, commercial and hazardous waste collection and disposal, energy consulting and resource recovery. Veolia helps commercial, industrial, healthcare, higher education and municipality customers throughout North America. Headquartered in Boston, Veolia has more than 10,000 employees working at more than 350 locations across North America.

Job Description

Position Purpose:

This position provides specialized transportation and disposal services to our clients. Responsibilities include manifesting, packaging, spill response, labeling, loading/unloading, coordinating transportation, and sampling/profiling material while adhering to state and federal environmental, health, and safety regulations.

Primary Duties /Responsibilities

Sample waste streams according to prescribed policies and procedures.Manifesting, packaging, spill clean-up response and remedial activities, labeling, loading, off loading, and coordinating transportation.Maintain a professional image to clients by answering inquiries and suggesting solutions to existing/potential problems.Comply and enforce all compliance, health, safety, and procedures in accordance with VES-TS policies.Become familiar with all U. S. Department of Transportation (DOT)/Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) and VES-TS regulations required in submitting paperwork to expedite disposal of hazardous waste.Operate and maintain all equipment in a professional manner to ensure optimum efficiency and effectiveness.Other duties as assigned.

Qualifications

Education / Experience / Background: 

High School diploma or General Educational Development (GED) requiredBachelor’s degree in Chemistry or a related science discipline preferred

Knowledge / Skills / Abilities

Strong team playerExcellent interpersonal and communication skillsTime management: the ability to organize and manage multiple deadlinesStrong customer service orientationComputer proficiencyAbility to follow through on assignments.

Required Certification / Licenses / Training

40-hour HAZWOPER CertificationValid Driver's LicenseAbility to obtain a Class A or B Commercial Drivers License (CDL) with Hazardous (H) endorsement

Additional Information

Benefits: Veolia’s comprehensive benefits package includes paid time off policies, as well as health, dental, vision, life insurance, savings accounts, tuition reimbursement, paid volunteering and more. In addition, employees are also entitled to participate in an employer sponsored 401(k) plan, to save for retirement.

We are an Equal Opportunity Employer! All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability or protected veteran status.

Disclaimer: The salary, other compensation, and benefits information is accurate as of the date of this posting. The Company reserves the right to modify this information at any time, subject to applicable law.
Qualifications
High School diploma or General Educational Development (GED) required
Knowledge / Skills / Abilities
Strong team player
Excellent interpersonal and communication skills
Time management: the ability to organize and manage multiple deadlines
Strong customer service orientation
Computer proficiency
Ability to follow through on assignments
Required Certification / Licenses / Training
40-hour HAZWOPER Certification
Valid Driver's License
Ability to obtain a Class A or B Commercial Drivers License (CDL) with Hazardous (H) endorsement
Benefits
Benefits: Veolia’s comprehensive benefits package includes paid time off policies, as well as health, dental, vision, life insurance, savings accounts, tuition reimbursement, paid volunteering and more
In addition, employees are also entitled to participate in an employer sponsored 401(k) plan, to save for retirement

This position provides specialized transportation and disposal services to our clients
Responsibilities include manifesting, packaging, spill response, labeling, loading/unloading, coordinating transportation, and sampling/profiling material while adhering to state and federal environmental, health, and safety regulations
Sample waste streams according to prescribed policies and procedures
Manifesting, packaging, spill clean-up response and remedial activities, labeling, loading, off loading, and coordinating transportation
Maintain a professional image to clients by answering inquiries and suggesting solutions to existing/potential problems

Become familiar with all U. S
Department of Transportation (DOT)/Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) and VES-TS regulations required in submitting paperwork to expedite disposal of hazardous waste
Operate and maintain all equipment in a professional manner to ensure optimum efficiency and effectiveness

jobs.title: Environmental Specialist
jobs.description:
SUMMARY 

Mortenson is currently seeking an Environmental Specialist with the ability to interact with a variety of staff at all levels in an ever-changing environment, remaining flexible, resourceful, proactive, and efficient with a high level of professionalism and confidentiality.  The Environmental Specialist will support Mortenson’s environmental management efforts for our Seattle and Portland commercial construction projects with potential to support additional commercial groups beyond the Pacific Northwest. This individual will be providing review of environmental documentation and advising on environmental matters in a manner consistent with company policies and procedures.

This individual can be based out of either our Portland, OR or Seattle, WA office.

RESPONSIBILITIES

Implement Mortenson’s environmental program for simple to moderately complex project sites and promulgate environmental compliance of construction activities.  Essential requirements:Develop site specific environmental plans and activitiesReview environmental submittals and attend preconstruction and preparatory meetingsImplement policies and procedures to ensure compliance with environmental regulationsDevelop and lead environmental orientation and training programsLead onsite best management practices for environmental managementMonitor implementation of environmental mitigation measures and conduct work site environmental inspectionsCollaborate with customers on regulatory (Federal, State, Provincial and local) reporting and documentation requirementsMaintain operational knowledge of environmental issues in construction, and other applicable standards, regulations, laws, and proceduresInterface with customer’s environmental personnel, environmental consultants, regulatory representatives, etc.Assist in the development company-wide Environmental Management supportive programs 

QUALIFICATIONS

Bachelor’s degree or higher in Geology, Environmental Management, Environmental Science, Biology, or related field Minimum of four years related experienceSignificant experience with two or more of the following areas: erosion prevention and sediment control, storm water pollution prevention plans, spill prevention controls and countermeasures, wetlands, endangered speciesStrong understanding of Federal, State, Provincial and local laws, policies and proceduresA well-organized and self-directed individual who is collaborative and a team playerAbility to interact with consultants and governmental agencies Ability to use PC, tablets and widely used software packages, e.g., spreadsheets (Excel), word processing (MS Word)Current driver’s licenseAble to travel up to 50%  

A few benefits offered include: 

(for Non-Craft & Non-Union Craft working 25+ hours / week)

Medical and prescription drug plans that includes no additional cost vision coverage Dental plan 401k retirement plan with a generous Mortenson match Paid time off, holidays, and other paid leaves Employer paid Life, AD&D, and disability insurance No-Cost mental health tool and concierge with extensive work-life resourcesTuition reimbursement Adoption Assistance Gym Membership Discount Program  

The base pay range for this role is $89,500 - $120,800. (Actual range is higher for the following office locations: Denver, CO and Chicago, IL – 5%, Seattle, WA, and Portland, OR – 10%).

Base pay is positioned within the range based on several factors including an individual’s knowledge, skills, and experience, with consideration given to internal equity.  This position is eligible for Mortenson’s incentive plan.

#LI-BS1

Please make note: 

Visa sponsorship is not offered for this position.Our postings are typically open a minimum of 5 days and an average of 44 days.  

ABOUT MORTENSON

As a top builder, developer, and EPC (Engineering, Procurement, and Construction), our expertise spans markets like sports, renewable energy, data centers, healthcare, and more. We are builders at heart, working to ensure the built environment has a lasting positive impact. 

Let’s Redefine Possible®  

Equal Employment Opportunity

Your uniqueness brings new and creative perspectives to the team. Mortenson is committed to providing equal opportunities of employment (EOE) to all individuals, regardless of your race, religion, gender, national origin, age, veteran status, disability, marital status or any other legally protected category.

Other Items to Note

Mortenson reserves the right to hire any individual without legal or financial obligation on unwanted solicitations. No agency emails, calls, or solicitations are accepted without a valid agreement.Must be currently legally authorized to work in the U.S. without sponsorship for employment visa status (e.g., H1B status, 0-1, TN, CPT, OPT, etc.). We are unable to sponsor or take over sponsorship of an employment Visa at this time.
Qualifications
Bachelor’s degree or higher in Geology, Environmental Management, Environmental Science, Biology, or related field
Minimum of four years related experience
Significant experience with two or more of the following areas: erosion prevention and sediment control, storm water pollution prevention plans, spill prevention controls and countermeasures, wetlands, endangered species
Strong understanding of Federal, State, Provincial and local laws, policies and procedures
A well-organized and self-directed individual who is collaborative and a team player
Ability to interact with consultants and governmental agencies
Ability to use PC, tablets and widely used software packages, e.g., spreadsheets (Excel), word processing (MS Word)
Current driver’s license
Able to travel up to 50%
Must be currently legally authorized to work in the U.S. without sponsorship for employment visa status (e.g., H1B status, 0-1, TN, CPT, OPT, etc.). We are unable to sponsor or take over sponsorship of an employment Visa at this time
Benefits
(for Non-Craft & Non-Union Craft working 25+ hours / week)
Medical and prescriptio
